original_hypothesis.title: Admin Drain of Unclaimed Agent Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized function allows the DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to withdraw any ERC20 token from the Genesis contract. This includes the agentTokenAddress, allowing the admin to drain tokens that users are entitled to but have not yet claimed.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Admin sweep functions must explicitly protect user funds. Allowing the admin to withdraw the exact asset that backs user claims creates a direct rug-pull vector.

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20(token).safeTransfer(to, amount); without checking against claimable balances

preconditions: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE is compromised or acts maliciously

operational_questions: Is the DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE strictly controlled by a timelocked multisig?
Is there a legitimate operational need to sweep agent tokens?
validation_requirements: Test withdrawing agent tokens while claims are pending
Verify claimAgentToken reverts due to insufficient balance afterward

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker waits for users to accumulate unclaimed agent tokens
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Attacker calls withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized targeting the agent token
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker drains the contract, leaving users unable to claim

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Theft of user funds, protocol insolvency for claims
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - funds must be returned manually

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Genesis contract balance drops below total unclaimed amount

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
